name: Pembrolizumab (Keytruda)
dose_regimen_cited: KEYTRUDA monotherapy across most approved solid-tumor indications (e.g., NSCLC, melanoma, HNSCC, HCC, MCC, cSCC): 200 mg IV every 3 weeks. (Label also lists an alternative fixed regimen of 400 mg IV every 6 weeks for the same indications; 200 mg Q3W was used as the primary/most commonly cited regimen.)

wac_source.description: Merck Colorado C.R.S. 12-280-308 WAC disclosure: KEYTRUDA 100 mg/4 mL (25 mg/mL) single-use vial (NDC 00006-3026-02), WAC = $6,136.00 per vial = $61.36/mg.

name: Nivolumab (Opdivo)
dose_regimen_cited: OPDIVO monotherapy across most approved solid-tumor indications: 240 mg IV every 2 weeks. (Label also lists alternative fixed regimens of 480 mg IV every 4 weeks, and a legacy weight-based option of 3 mg/kg IV every 2 weeks for some indications; 240 mg Q2W was used as the primary regimen.)

wac_source.description: Bristol Myers Squibb Colorado HB 19-1131 pricing disclosure: OPDIVO 40 mg/4 mL vial WAC = $1,323.85/vial (= $33.09625/mg); consistent across all vial sizes listed (100 mg/10 mL = $3,309.60; 120 mg/12 mL = $3,971.53; 240 mg/24 mL = $7,943.08), all ~$33.096/mg.

name: Atezolizumab (Tecentriq)
dose_regimen_cited: TECENTRIQ monotherapy across most approved solid-tumor indications: 1200 mg IV every 3 weeks. (Label also lists alternative fixed regimens of 840 mg IV every 2 weeks and 1680 mg IV every 4 weeks; 1200 mg Q3W was used as the primary/most commonly cited regimen.)

wac_source.description: Genentech Colorado HB 19-1131 pricing disclosure (source: AnalySource/First Databank, 07/01/2026): TECENTRIQ 840 mg/14 mL vial WAC = $8,606.62/vial (= $10.24598/mg, WAC unit price $614.75857/mL); 1,200 mg/20 mL vial WAC = $12,295.15/vial (~$10.246/mg).

name: Ipilimumab (Yervoy)
dose_regimen_cited: YERVOY monotherapy for unresectable or metastatic melanoma: 3 mg/kg IV every 3 weeks for a maximum of 4 doses (Section 2.2, Recommended Dosage, Table 1).

wac_source.description: Bristol Myers Squibb Colorado HB 19-1131 pricing disclosure (shared portfolio pricing document with OPDIVO): YERVOY 50 mg/10 mL vial WAC = $9,054.74/vial (= $181.0948/mg), consistent with the 200 mg/40 mL vial WAC of $36,218.89 (~$181.09/mg).

name: Durvalumab (Imfinzi)
dose_regimen_cited: IMFINZI monotherapy maintenance dosing for patients weighing 30 kg or more (e.g., limited-stage SCLC maintenance, biliary tract cancer, endometrial cancer, HCC in combination regimens): 1500 mg IV every 4 weeks (Section 2.2). (Label also lists a weight-based regimen of 10 mg/kg IV every 2 weeks used for unresectable Stage III NSCLC durvalumab monotherapy and for patients under 30 kg; 1500 mg fixed dosing was used as the primary regimen for this record.)

wac_source.reason: No current, primary manufacturer or state WAC disclosure could be located. AstraZeneca's Colorado/Vermont price-transparency disclosure pages returned HTTP 403 (access blocked) on all attempts, and no AstraZeneca WAC pricing-sheet PDF for Imfinzi (analogous to the Merck/Genentech/BMS Colorado HB 19-1131 disclosures found for the other four drugs) could be found via search. The only explicit 'WAC' figure found for durvalumab ($73.30 per 10-mg HCPCS unit) originates from a 2020 Oncology Practice Management coding-and-pricing table and is too stale/unverifiable as a current benchmark, so it was not used. Third-party listings found (drugpatentwatch.com, ndclist.com, pipelinemedical.com, 1mg.com) were either paywalled, mismatched to the wrong NDC, or reflected distributor/cash pricing rather than confirmed manufacturer WAC, so they were excluded per instructions not to substitute a cash/discount price for WAC.
